heading: Can I migrate between regions?
body:
Yes. You can change one or more regions for a given cluster within the
original cloud service provider or across different cloud service providers.
MongoDB Atlas uses a rolling-migration strategy for moving nodes from the
original region to a new region to preserve cluster availability.

> **Important:** 
>
> AWS Only
>
> Amazon Web Services (AWS) Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) peering
> connections are region-specific. Clusters utilizing an existing
> VPC (Virtual Private Cloud) peering connection to an AWS (Amazon Web Services) VPC (Virtual Private Cloud) in a given AWS (Amazon Web Services) region
> lose access to that peering connection if moved to a different AWS (Amazon Web Services)
> region. The moved cluster may use an existing peering connection in
> the new region.
>
> To learn more, see Set Up a Network Peering Connection.

If you need to migrate data between regions on different cloud service
providers, you can:

- [edit the cluster configuration](edit-cluster-atlas) in the Atlas UI by changing the cloud providers and region for each cluster whose data you are migrating. The migration rolls cluster by cluster, starting from the secondary nodes.

- [Configure High Availability and Workload Isolation](create-cluster-multi-region). This method minimizes the risk of issues related to cloud provider capacity, but can result in higher [data transfer costs](data-transfer-costs).

- Seed the destination cluster using mongorestore.

- Restore a backup from the source cluster to the destination cluster.

> **Warning:** 
>
> - When you transition to a new cloud provider, your deployment IP addresses change.
>
> - Data migration interrupts VPC peering and private endpoint operation. You must reconfigure these after completing migration.
>
> - Adding or moving a node to a new region or cloud provider without a primary or secondary node
>   requires each newly migrated replica set member to perform an [initial sync](manual/core/replica-set-sync).
>
> - If you created an Atlas cluster on Google Cloud or Microsoft Azure before November 2, 2020 when Atlas added support for
>   multi-cloud clusters, changing to a different provider changes the connection string to your new cluster. Consider
>   scheduling a time to [update your applications with the new connection string](tenant-to-tenant) to connect to the cluster again.

> **See Also:** [Migrate or Import Data](import-strategies)
